plan: We will trim SPY to reduce concentration and meet the mandate.
All leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) will be sold to zero for capital preservation.
SMH will be trimmed due to its downtrend, reducing semiconductor exposure.
QQQI will be increased to favor income and broad index exposure.
A significant cash buffer will be established to enhance safety and reduce overall risk.
These moves aim for a stable portfolio with minimal drawdown.
conservative.read: This conservative plan targets a modest ~1.5%/month return, prioritizing capital preservation. It significantly reduces leverage and concentration, aiming for lower volatility and minimal drawdown.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to comply with the single-name concentration mandate.
SOXL will be sold entirely as it is below its stop and a leveraged ETF.
SPAL will be trimmed to reduce overall leveraged ETF exposure.
SMH will be trimmed due to its downtrend, balancing sector risk.
CRWD will be increased, leaning into a strong performing AI/Software name.
AMD will be bought as a new position, based on its high scanner score.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for ~2.5%/month growth, focusing on risk-first management. It reduces overall leverage and concentration while blending broad index exposure with quality tech and new high-scoring setups.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to comply with the single-name concentration mandate.
SOXL will be sold entirely as it is below its stop, despite the aggressive mode.
SPAL will be trimmed slightly, keeping leveraged ETF exposure within limits.
SMH will be trimmed due to its downtrend, focusing on working setups.
CRWD will be significantly increased, pressing into a strong performer.
AMD will be bought as a new position, leveraging its high scanner score.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets ~4%/month growth, leaning into high-scoring setups and leverage. It carries significantly higher drawdown risk due to concentrated positions and market volatility. Leveraged ETFs also experience daily decay, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: SPY will be trimmed to reduce concentration and free up capital for income.
All leveraged ETFs (SOXL, SPAL) will be sold to zero, prioritizing stability.
SMH will be sold entirely due to its downtrend and high-beta nature.
CRWD will be trimmed to reduce exposure to high-beta single names.
QQQI will be significantly increased to maximize monthly distributions.
A substantial cash buffer will be established to prioritize low drawdown and stability.
income.read: This income plan targets ~1%/month, prioritizing steady monthly distributions and low drawdown. While aiming for stability, distributions are variable, and NAV can still fall in selloffs. This is not a savings account.
